titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 735

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=980 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.552821+00 | translation moa=981 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.543373+00

p.cl: Omnibus Has Literas visuris vel audituris . P . filius Comitis . P . de Dumbar Salutem .

Nouerit vniuersitas
vestra Me Anno ab incarnatione Domini . M . c c . xxx . ii . Die Sancti Iohannis baptiste Recepisse a Priore
de Coldigham et conuentu Ducentas Marcas argenti in quarum persolucione . predicti prior et conuentus
per quieta clamatione mea Eisdem facta de villa de Swinewde et Dominio cum omnibus pertinenciis
suis pro me et heredibus meis in perpetuum Mihi obligati fuerunt .

In huius autem receptionis
testimonium huic scripto Sigillum meum apposui .
To all who shall view or hear these letters, Patrick son of Earl Patrick of Dunbar sends greeting.

Know me, all of you, in the 1232nd year after the Lord's incarnation, on Saint John the Baptist's day [24th June], to have received from the prior and convent of Coldingham two hundred merks of silver, in payment of which the aforesaid prior and convent were obliged to me in return for my own quitclaim to the same made in respect of the toun of Swinewood and the demesne with all its pertinents for myself and my heirs for ever.
As witness of this receipt I have applied my seal to this writing.
